deepseek-r1-distill-llama-8b.text: Okay, so I'm trying to figure out the diagnosis for this 19-year-old patient.
Let me go through the case step by step.
The patient presented with otalgia, slurred speech, and ataxia.
He had a history of fatigue, fever, pharyngitis, and lymphadenopathy a month before admission.

Initially, he was diagnosed with infectious mononucleosis due to EBV, but then developed more severe symptoms like dysphagia, pain, and hearing loss in the right ear, leading to a diagnosis of otitis media.
He was treated with prednisone, amoxicillin, and azithromycin.
However, his symptoms didn't improve, and he developed severe otalgia again with a right tympanic membrane perforation and purulent discharge.
He was prescribed cefuroxime and topical ciprofloxacin/dexamethasone.
Despite this, he started having unsteadiness and fell several times, leading to an MRI which showed opacification in the right mastoid air cells and middle ear, consistent with a tympanostomy.
After some treatment changes, including switching to vancomycin and ceftriaxone, his symptoms persisted.
On further evaluation, he had slurred speech, clumsiness, and gait imbalance.
Neurological exams showed mild anomia, impaired verbal fluency, and executive dysfunction, suggesting possible prefrontal cortex issues.
He had hypotonia, dysdiadochokinesia, and ataxia.
His MRI showed no brain abnormalities, but the ear issues were still present.
Given the history of viral symptoms (like mononucleosis) and the neurological findings, especially the cerebellar signs like ataxia and dysmetria, I'm thinking of viral encephalitis.
EBV is a common cause of encephalitis, especially in young adults.
The presence of otitis media could be a secondary infection, but the primary issue seems to be the encephalitis.
The MRI didn't show brain lesions, which is common in acute cases, as the inflammation might not have been chronic enough to show up yet.
The treatment with antivirals and antibiotics aligns with viral encephalitis management.
So, the most likely diagnosis is Epstein-Barr virus encephalitis.
